name: Colourful Venus Beach Retreat
subtitle: Pool & 2 Terraces
welcomeMessage: Your vibrant, colourful home with pool and two terraces, all set for you. Relax, explore, and soak up the Mediterranean. We're just a message away.

address.nearestLandmark: Behind AVLIDA Hotel, Tombs of the Kings area

checkin.keyLocation: Two key boxes on the left wall, under Post Box 'D6'. Open the one on the right
steps: Walk to the pool area at the back of the King's Park complex
Pass the pool and turn right along the pool's fence
Turn left at the first stone arch passage
Find the two key boxes on the left wall, under the Post Box labelled 'D7'
Open the key box on the right using code 9007
Go through the corridor. The apartment is the first door on the right (ground floor)
Once inside, place the metallic key in the key plug next to the door to switch on the electricity (just like in hotels)
Return the key to the box when you check out and reset the code
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding us, call or WhatsApp anytime, we're happy to guide you step by step.

gettingHere.byCar: Follow the Google Maps link, it navigates directly inside the complex to the pool area. Park anywhere, then use the photo guide below to reach the apartment.
gettingHere.parking: Free parking at the pool area, spaces freely available. Apartment entrance is a 1-min walk. Avoid parking under trees on windy days.
gettingHere.publicTransport: From Paphos Airport, take Bus 612 and get off at the Tombs of the Kings bus station. From there you have two options: walk (~18 min, 1.3 km) or hop on Bus 615 for one stop to get a bit closer, then walk the rest (~4 min). On foot: head toward the AVLIDA Hotel → walk past it → turn right at the Esso gas station → the King's Park complex entrance is just behind it. ⚠️ Buses only accept cash. €2.50 per ride per person.

directionPhotos.caption: Turn right at the Esso gas station (into Morphou street)
Go up around 50m to the big construction on the right
Keep going, just a bit more...
At this construction, turn right into the narrow street
Park here or continue on foot past the pool
Follow the road to the back where the pool area is
Turn left at the first stone arch passage
Two key boxes under Post Box 'D7'. Open the one on the right. Code: 9007
After getting the keys, walk through the passage and turn right
The first apartment on the right hand side is yours 😊

gettingHere.taxi: Taxis wait outside arrivals at Paphos Airport. Fare: €40–45 (day), €45–50 (after hours). ~20–25 min. A slightly cheaper option is to order a Bolt ride.

houseRules: No smoking inside, balconies and terraces only
No parties, events or loud music. Quiet hours 10pm to 8am
Keep air conditioning doors and windows closed when in use
Make sure to ventilate the apartment regularly while you are inside. Open windows and doors when the AC is off
Any breakages must be reported, we understand accidents happen
Return keys to the keybox and reset the code at checkout. Lost or locked-in keys will require a locksmith call-out at your cost.

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: Place the metallic key in the key plug by the door to power the apartment. Take it when you leave. Each socket has its own wall switch, if something isn't working, check it's turned on. EU adapters provided.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: Electric stove, oven, coffee maker and grinder. Salt, pepper, olive oil, and coffee beans provided, help yourself. ⚠️ Clean dishes promptly and keep counters clear, warm weather attracts insects.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: In the kitchen. Quick Wash works for most loads.

appliances.name: Shower & Hot Water
appliances.instructions: Takes 2–3 min to warm up, normal. If no hot water, press the heating button next to the bathroom door and wait 20 min. Turn off the heater before stepping in.

appliances.name: Pool Access
appliances.instructions: Pool key is on your keychain. No glass, no diving from the edge. No pets in the pool. Be mindful of other guests. Use the pool towels from the sun lounger basket, not the bathroom towels. Rubbish bins are in the parking area, on your right as you walk out past the pool.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: Towels, hair dryer, and basic toiletries provided. ⚠️ Use the bin next to the toilet, do not flush toilet paper.

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water is safe, but many prefer bottled. Available at nearby supermarkets.

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: Water every 3–4 days if staying longer, they'll appreciate it!

items.name: Venus Beach
items.tip: Closest useful beach. 15-20 min on foot. Quick swim, sunset, convenience.

items.name: Faros Beach
items.tip: 25-35 min on foot or a short drive. Sunset, casual swim.

items.name: Sandy Beach, Chloraka
items.tip: Less touristy, local feel. Good for sunset. Better when the sea is calm.

items.name: Municipal Baths, Kato Paphos
items.tip: City swimming spot, not a sandy beach. Handy for a quick dip near the promenade.

items.name: Alykes Beach, Kato Paphos
items.tip: Organised beach with facilities, good for families. Easier by car or bus than on foot.

items.name: Coral Bay Beach
items.tip: The safest best beach choice near Paphos. Sandy, organised, easy. Hard to go wrong.

items.name: Laourou / Corallia Beach
items.tip: My preferred alternative to Coral Bay. Similar convenience, usually calmer.

items.name: Sea Caves, Peyia
items.tip: Dramatic coastline. Better for scenery and sunset photos than a classic beach day.

items.name: Agios Georgios Beach, Peyia
items.tip: Small beach, good for snorkelling. Combine with Sea Caves and Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: White River Beach / Toxeftra
items.tip: Raw and beautiful, wild coast. Better for exploring than sunbeds.

items.name: Lara Beach / Lara Bay
items.tip: One of the most special beaches in the Paphos area. Turtle nesting site. Rough access, no facilities.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: Legendary birthplace of Aphrodite. Beautiful scenery and photos, but rocky and not always ideal for swimming.

items.name: Latchi Harbour (Blue Lagoon boat trips)
items.tip: Best starting point for Blue Lagoon without off-road driving. Boat trips depart from here.

items.name: Blue Lagoon, Akamas
items.tip: Most impressive water in the Paphos area. Not a drive-up beach. Take a boat from Latchi.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.tip: Closest and most important historical site. Rock-cut tombs from the Hellenistic and Roman periods, part of the UNESCO listed Paphos archaeological area.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Walk along the coastline toward Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle. Easy and scenic, especially at sunset.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach & Lighthouse
items.tip: Relaxed halfway point between Avlida and the harbour. Good for a scenic walk with a beach stop.

items.name: Old Town Paphos & Kennedy Square
items.tip: Good for cafes, local streets, small shops, and a more authentic Paphos feel. Best visited late afternoon.

items.name: Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
items.tip: Top cultural site in Cyprus. Roman mosaics considered among the finest in the eastern Mediterranean, UNESCO listed.

items.name: Paphos Harbour & Castle
items.tip: Easy evening plan. Byzantine fort rebuilt by the Lusignans, then Venetians, then Ottomans in the 16th century.

items.name: Agios Neophytos Monastery
items.tip: Beautiful half-day stop. Founded in the 12th century by Saint Neophytos in a secluded valley north of Paphos.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Scenic coast, great for photos and sunset. Combine with Oniro by the Sea and Agios Georgios.

items.name: Agios Georgios Archaeological Site
items.tip: Quiet ruins with sea views, off the tourist trail. Good if already heading toward Sea Caves or Lara.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: Most iconic scenic stop in Cyprus. Better for views and mythology than swimming.

items.name: Akamas Peninsula
items.tip: Wild nature, viewpoints, walking trails. Road conditions vary. Best for adventurous guests.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: Shopping, food court, cinema. Practical for an indoor break on hot or very windy days.

items.name: Paphos Harbour
items.tip: One of the easiest evening plans from Avlida. Dinner, drinks, and a relaxed walk by the sea.

items.name: Paphos Aphrodite Waterpark
items.tip: Best family attraction nearby. Easy half-day or full-day option on hot days.

items.name: Coral Bay & Corallia Beach
items.tip: Easiest classic beach excursion. Sand, sunbeds, and facilities. Hard to go wrong.

items.name: Horse Riding
items.tip: Nice alternative to beach and history. Book in advance, especially in high season.

items.name: Adonis Baths Waterfalls
items.tip: Popular excursion, road can be rough. Better for adventurous guests than polished sightseeing.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Easy scenic excursion. Great for photos and sunset. Combine with Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Paphos Zoo
items.tip: Good family option near Coral Bay. Easy to combine with Sea Caves or a beach day.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: One of the best nature walks in the region. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain or extreme heat.

items.name: Wine Villages around Kathikas
items.tip: Great for adults wanting a local experience. Better with a tour if everyone is tasting.

items.name: Akamas Jeep Safari
items.tip: Best for guests who want wild nature without driving difficult roads themselves. One of the best preserved areas in Cyprus.

items.name: Polis & Latchi
items.tip: Calmer, more local side of Cyprus. Good for lunch, a coastal walk, and Blue Lagoon boat departures.

items.name: Blue Lagoon Boat Trip from Latchi
items.tip: One of the best full-day excursions from Paphos. Many tours combine with Akamas and Baths of Aphrodite.

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite
items.tip: Small site, but beautiful surroundings. Best combined with Latchi, Blue Lagoon, and Polis.

items.name: Omodos Village
items.tip: One of the prettiest village excursions from Paphos. Works well as part of a Troodos or wine route day.

items.name: Kourion Archaeological Site
items.tip: Excellent day trip toward Limassol. Ancient theatre with sea views is the highlight.

items.name: Limassol Marina & Old Town
items.tip: More urban and polished than Paphos. Best combined with Kourion or Omodos.

items.name: Troodos Mountains & Villages
items.tip: Best full-day escape from the coast. Villages, monasteries, wine routes, and cooler air.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Best no-car option. Walk from the Tombs of the Kings area toward Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach Coastal Walk
items.tip: Practical from Avlida. Good for guests who want nature without a proper hike.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings coastal loop
items.tip: Combines ancient tombs, open landscape, and coastline. Not a formal trail, but a very nice loop.

items.name: Sea Caves coastal walk, Peyia
items.tip: Easy scenic outing. Combine with Edro III Shipwreck and Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Avakas Gorge Nature Trail
items.tip: Best nature trail near Paphos. Follows Avgas River through limestone formations. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock coastal walk
items.tip: Scenic stop, not a long hike. Good when driving toward Limassol or the airport.

items.name: Lara Beach & Toxeftra coastal area
items.tip: Beautiful but rough access. Wild coast and turtle nesting areas. Respect the environment.

items.name: Smigies Nature Trail, Neo Chorio
items.tip: Less demanding than Aphrodite or Adonis. Short route 2.5 km (1hr), long route 6 km (2hrs).

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite short walk
items.tip: Gentle nature stop, not a real hike. Best combined with Latchi or a Blue Lagoon boat trip.

items.name: Aphrodite Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: One of the most beautiful hikes in the region. Wide views over Polis, Latchi, and Akamas.

items.name: Adonis Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: Views over Polis Chrysochous, Paphos Forest, and Akamas. Connects with Smigies and Aphrodite trails.

items.name: Troodos Nature Trails
items.tip: Best full-day hiking option in summer when the coast is too hot. Cooler forest trails and mountain scenery.

items.name: Karlina Restaurant
items.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

items.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
items.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

items.name: Tea for Two
items.tip: Good for an easy breakfast or lunch close to home. Friendly service and great value.

items.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
items.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

items.name: Kingfisher Taverna
items.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

items.name: Three Little Pigs
items.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall Food Court
items.tip: Not special, but very practical. Good for families, quick food, coffee, or escaping the heat.

items.name: Old Town Paphos cafés
items.tip: More local feeling than the harbour. Nice for brunch, coffee, or early evening drinks.

items.name: Pallia Ilektriki
items.tip: A good Old Town option. Ranked among popular places near Old Town Paphos.

items.name: Ta Pinakia
items.tip: Good if you want something less harbour-focused. Well-rated near Old Town Paphos.

items.name: O Firis
items.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions. Live music almost every evening. Book ahead!

items.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
items.tip: Touristy but reliable. Many options for dinner, drinks, and a sunset walk by the sea.

items.name: King's Restaurant
items.tip: Popular harbour area choice. Good fish and Cypriot food.

items.name: Theo's Restaurant
items.tip: Good for seafood near the harbour. Sea bass, octopus, prawns, and calamari.

items.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
items.tip: More about the harbour atmosphere than fine dining. Easy and relaxed.

items.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
items.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood, steps from the sea.

items.name: Oniro by the Sea
items.tip: One of the best scenic spots in the Paphos area. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III shipwreck.

items.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
items.tip: More authentic than the seafront. Best for a longer lunch or early dinner with wine tasting.

items.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
items.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or a Polis day trip.

items.name: Lidl
items.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

items.name: AlphaMega
items.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

items.name: Paphos Market
items.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

items.name: Old Town
items.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: Central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is 18 min walk (~1.3 km) away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car-sharing app that works well around Paphos. Sign up in minutes. Cars typically within a 5–10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly ride app. Good alternative to metered taxis around Paphos.

gettingAround.name: Taxi
gettingAround.info: Various taxi services are available online. One reliable option is Paphos Taxi Airport, where you can also arrange a pickup directly via WhatsApp: +357 99 460 829.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Paphos District Hospital
contacts.note: Main hospital in Paphos, approx. 10 min drive

emergency.nearestHospital: Paphos District Hospital, approx. 10 min drive.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. Ask us if you need a recommendation.

steps: Strip the bed linens and leave them piled on the bed
Wash up any dishes used and leave them on the drying rack
Clear the fridge and dispose of any leftover food
Take all rubbish bags to the bins in the parking area. As you walk out, they are on your right hand side after the pool
Close all windows, doors and shutters
Switch off all lights, AC, and the TV
Lock the apartment door and return all keys to the keybox, reset the code after
Drop us a WhatsApp to let us know you've left, and we'd love a review! 😊
checkout.lateCheckout: We try to be flexible. If you'd like a late checkout, please let us know 1-2 days before your departure and we'll do our best to arrange it.
